tests.name: init_sh_non_interactive_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh --non-interactive completed Setup Complete banner; init log saved to .solo-orchestrator/init-20260425-210936.log

tests.name: context7_detection
tests.details: verify-install.sh from project dir reports Context7 MCP installed and configured

tests.name: framework_self_guard_active
tests.details: Running init.sh --non-interactive from $FRAMEWORK exits 1 with 'Refusing to operate inside the Solo Orchestrator framework repo' (PR #18 fix holds)

tests.name: baseline_build_to_phase2_minimum
tests.details: Started feature uat-feat-1, completed all 6 build_loop steps (tests_written, tests_verified_failing, implemented, security_audit, documentation_updated, feature_recorded). Security audit stubbed at docs/security-audits/uat-feat-1-security-audit.md. Commit a feat(uat-1) succeeded; test-gate.sh --record-feature recorded the feature; build-progress.json features_completed contains uat-feat-1.

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: bash scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational exits RC=1 due to a NEW bug in upgrade-project.sh helper-refresh block: cp source==destination when invoked from project-local script. Upgrade core (deployment field write, CLAUDE.md/PROJECT_INTAKE.md/APPROVAL_LOG.md updates, git commit) DOES complete before the abort. See framework_bugs for both the new helper-refresh failure and U-G confirmation when invoking from $FRAMEWORK directly.

tests.name: process_state_preserved
tests.details: .build_loop history preserved; build-progress.features_completed still contains uat-feat-1 after upgrade

tests.name: new_required_artifacts_listed
tests.details: Upgrade output lists: 1) Pre-Phase 0 organizational pre-conditions in APPROVAL_LOG.md; 2) Section 8 of PROJECT_INTAKE.md (governance pre-flight); 3) Assign approval authorities for each phase gate

tests.name: pending_approval_sentinel_respected
tests.details: After writing .claude/pending-approval.json, intake-wizard --upgrade-deployment refuses with '[FAIL] upgrade blocked — pending user decision' and exits non-zero. PR #17 U-F fix holds.

tests.name: upgrade_changelog_present_in_script
tests.details: scripts/upgrade-project.sh header has Changelog section with BL-006 (2026-04-24), BL-015 (2026-04-25), BL-016 (2026-04-25) entries

tests.name: no_template_placeholders_in_output
tests.details: grep -E '__PROJECT_NAME__|__PLATFORM__|__LANGUAGE__|__TRACK__' against upgrade log returned no matches; U-I not regressed in this config

tests.name: deployment_field_written_correctly
tests.details: U-J confirmation: phase-state.json .deployment is still 'personal' after upgrade despite output saying 'personal -> organizational'. tool-preferences.json track was updated but deployment field on phase-state.json remained 'personal'.

tests.name: u_d_phase_advance
tests.details: U-D confirmation: after Phase 2 build-loop completion no script auto-advances .current_phase from 0; remained 0 throughout test.

tests.name: u_g_intake_wizard_project_root
tests.details: U-G confirmation: invoking $FRAMEWORK/scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational from the project directory fails with 'PROJECT_INTAKE.md not found' because PROJECT_ROOT=$(cd $SCRIPT_DIR/.. && pwd) hardcodes to framework directory. Source comment at scripts/intake-wizard.sh:16-18 explicitly acknowledges U-G is deferred to Batch 3. Worked around by invoking the project-local copy at scripts/intake-wizard.sh.

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh helper-refresh fails when invoked via project-local script (cp source==dest)
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash $PROJECT_DIR/scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational  (which delegates to upgrade-project.sh). At line ~1473 the loop runs cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/$helper" "scripts/$helper" where SCRIPT_DIR is the project-local scripts/ directory; macOS cp returns non-zero with 'are identical (not copied)' and set -euo pipefail aborts the script.
framework_bugs.expected: Helper-refresh should be a no-op when source and destination resolve to the same file; upgrade should still exit 0
framework_bugs.actual: Script exits 1 mid-flow at the helper-refresh step. Upgrade core completes (deployment metadata written, commit made) but post-upgrade validation never runs and the user sees a confusing cp error. Affects every project that runs the project-local intake-wizard upgrade path. Note: invoking $FRAMEWORK/scripts/upgrade-project.sh directly would not hit this because SCRIPT_DIR there is the framework path; but the project-local invocation is the user-facing flow.

framework_bugs.summary: U-G confirmation: intake-wizard PROJECT_ROOT hardcoded to framework via SCRIPT_DIR/..
framework_bugs.reproduction: From project dir: bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational  -> 'Error: PROJECT_INTAKE.md not found'
framework_bugs.expected: PROJECT_ROOT should resolve from $PWD or from a known sentinel (CLAUDE.md/.claude/) when invoked from a project
framework_bugs.actual: PROJECT_ROOT=$(cd $SCRIPT_DIR/.. && pwd) resolves to /Users/karl/Documents/Claude Projects/solo-orchestrator (framework). intake-wizard.sh:16-18 acknowledges this is deferred. Re-confirmed in this sweep.

framework_bugs.summary: U-J confirmation: deployment field not written to phase-state.json on upgrade
framework_bugs.reproduction: Run upgrade personal -> organizational via intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational. Inspect .claude/phase-state.json afterward.
framework_bugs.actual: .deployment remains 'personal' although last_upgrade is set to 2026-04-25 and tool-preferences.json track was updated. The upgrade output even logs 'Updated phase-state.json' but the deployment string is not actually rewritten in the JSON. Re-confirmed in this sweep.

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script auto-advances current_phase after Phase 2 build-loop completion
framework_bugs.reproduction: Run init.sh --non-interactive (.current_phase=0). Complete a full build_loop including feature_recorded. Inspect phase-state.json.
framework_bugs.expected: Some path should advance .current_phase from 0 to 2 (or at minimum 1) after gate prerequisites are met.
framework_bugs.actual: .current_phase remained 0 throughout. Re-confirmed in this sweep.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook Section F instruction 'intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment {TARGET_DEPLOYMENT}' does not advance poc_mode (still null after upgrade) — for a private_poc target, an additional --gov-mode style flag would be needed but the wizard does not expose one in --help.
documentation_gaps.where: RUNBOOK.md Section F upgrade flow vs. scripts/intake-wizard.sh --help (no --upgrade-poc-mode or equivalent)

documentation_gaps.gap: intake-wizard.sh --help lists only --upgrade-track and --upgrade-deployment; no flag to set poc_mode/gov_mode on an upgrade. Users upgrading personal -> private_poc cannot complete the metadata transition with the documented commands.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: grep for password/api_key/secret/token in committed files returns only documentation/template references (PROJECT_INTAKE.md tool table, evaluation-prompts security audit prompts). No actual credentials.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: .claude/process-audit.log absent (no FORCE/reset events) — N/A per runbook

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK rev-parse HEAD = d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494 (matches setup-time SHA). git -C $FRAMEWORK status --short shows only pre-existing untracked .claude/ and Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ which were already untracked at start of run; no tracked files modified.

summary: Section A all-pass (PR #17/#18/#19 fixes hold; non-interactive init clean). Upgrade flow surfaces one new High-severity bug (upgrade-project.sh helper-refresh aborts on macOS cp source==dest when invoked via project-local intake-wizard) plus three confirmations of still-open prior-sweep bugs (U-D, U-G, U-J). Pending-approval sentinel (U-F fix) and BL-006/BL-015 changelog text in upgrade-project.sh both verified. poc_mode never reaches private_poc because intake-wizard lacks a flag for it.
